commit.message:
Sea routes require 95%+ water tiles in all code paths

Fixed 4 locations where sea routes were created:
- generateSeaRoutes() primary loop: already had 95% check (no change)
- generateSeaRoutes() fallback loop: was skipping water check entirely
- Town founding (runtime): was using 30% threshold via checkWaterPath()
- buildNewSeaRoute(): had no water validation at all
- Post-load orphan port fixup: was creating routes with no water check

All now use findTerrainPath() with waterFraction >= 0.95 threshold.
Routes that fail validation are simply not created — ports without
enough open water between them won't get fake sea routes over land.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase-isolated RNG for world generation stability

World generation now uses independent sub-RNGs per phase (terrain,
kingdoms, towns, roads, sea routes, people), each derived from the
master seed with unique offsets. This ensures that changes to one
generation phase (e.g. adding king personality traits) never shift
the RNG sequence for other phases (e.g. town names).

Fixes tutorial seed 7777 producing different starting towns as the
codebase evolved. The master world.rng is still available for runtime
tick-based RNG usage.

Fix CSS loading issue: Move all styles to index.css, disable Tailwind

ROOT CAUSE: CSS styles were not being applied because:
1. main.tsx imported index.css which only had @tailwind directives
2. App.tsx imported App.css but main.tsx doesn't import it
3. PostCSS was configured to run Tailwind, stripping out CSS

SOLUTION:
1. Moved all CSS from App.css to index.css

3. Removed tailwindcss plugin from postcss.config.js
4. Now using only autoprefixer in PostCSS

commit.message:
NPC health/illness system: contagion, king AI, player exposure

Full NPC health system wired up (config already existed, logic was missing):

Illness generation (gentle rates):
- Minor (cold, flu, food poisoning): ~10% of population/year
- Moderate (fever, dysentery): ~5% of population/year
- Serious (pneumonia, typhus): ~1% of population/year
- Seasonal modifiers: winter 3x, autumn 1.5x
- Town modifiers: hospitals -50%, clinics -25%, wells -15%
- Prosperity and population density affect rates

Contagion system:
- Within-town: cold/flu slow, plague moderate spread
- Cross-town via roads: much slower, distance-based (longer = slower)
- Cross-town via sea routes: 1.4x worse (cramped ships), distance-based
- Smaller towns spread slower, higher prosperity slows spread
- ~10% deterministic immunity per person per illness
- ~20% asymptomatic carriers (spread without symptoms)

NPC illness progression:
- Health drains based on illness severity
- Hospitals/clinics greatly reduce drain and speed recovery
- Medical NPCs self-treat by buying supplies from market
- Non-medical NPCs also seek market remedies
- Death when health reaches 0 (logged to event system)
- Asymptomatic carriers recover with no health loss

Plague events overhauled:
- plague/plague_disaster now infect people instead of instant-killing
- Deaths come naturally from illness progression (untreated severe)
- Creates realistic spread rather than arbitrary daily kills

King AI health policy system:
- Checks kingdom-wide sickness every 7 days
- Proactive kings (high justice/intelligence) act earlier
- Greedy kings resist spending, wait longer to act
- Policy escalation: medical funding → public hygiene → quarantine → martial quarantine
- Port closure for port towns with plague
- DIAL-DOWN: policies relaxed/lifted as sickness subsides
  - <2% sick: all policies lifted
  - <5% sick: martial quarantine → regular quarantine → medical funding
- Policies cost daily treasury gold; auto-expire if kingdom goes broke
- Quarantine blocks cross-town contagion spread
- Event log messages for all policy changes

Player exposure:
- Daily check when in a town with contagious sick NPCs
- Risk scales with sick ratio in town
- Housing and medical skills provide protection
- Can contract cold or plague from town exposure

Save/load:
- NPC health fields (health, sick, illness, illnessDay) backfill on old saves
- Kingdom healthPolicies array serialized/deserialized
- NPC medical self-treatment code updated to use actual sick status

* fix: slice additionalInput at cursorPosition in immediate layer

The editor:cursor-window chunk in the immediate layer was using
additionalInput verbatim, completely ignoring cursorPosition.
This meant that builtin:continue calls with different cursor
positions but the same document text always produced identical
context/prompt windows—the core bug reported in PR #40 audit A.

Root cause (layerAssemblyService.ts):
  Before: content = additionalInput?.trim() ?? `cursor=${N}`
  After:  content = additionalInput.slice(0, cursorPosition)
          (falls back to cursor=N when preceding text is empty)

Why this is correct:
- For builtin:continue, additionalInput IS the full document text
  and cursorPosition is the write point within that text.
  The immediate layer should expose only the text BEFORE the cursor
  so the AI sees the preceding narrative context, not the whole doc.
- For polish/rewrite, selection.to (used as cursorPosition) is
  always >= additionalInput.length (selected text is shorter than
  its doc position), so Math.min clamps to text.length and the
  full selected text is preserved—no behavioral change for those
  skills.

Tests added (layerAssemblyService.cursor-window.test.ts):
- cursorPosition=3 with additionalInput='甲乙丙丁' → immediate='甲乙丙'
- cursorPosition=0 vs cursorPosition=3 → prompts are NOT equal
- cursorPosition=0 with text → fallback to 'cursor=0'
- cursorPosition > text.length → full text used (clamped)

Introduce explicit textOffset (plain-text chars) alongside cursorPosition
(ProseMirror doc position) in ContextAssembleRequest.  The immediate layer
fetcher now uses textOffset for text slicing when the IPC layer provides it,
while documentWriteback continues to use the raw PM position for insertion.

Root cause: layerAssemblyService immediate fetcher did text.slice(0, cursorPosition)
treating cursorPosition as a plain-text offset, but the frontend sends a
ProseMirror document position (which counts node boundary markers).  For a
single-paragraph doc 'ABCD', PM pos 3 = after 2nd char (text offset 2), not
after 3rd char.  This caused the context window to misalign with the actual
insertion anchor — the AI saw N chars before cursor but writeback inserted
at a different point.

  cursorPosition for text slicing (backwards-compat fallback preserved)

  PM document from DB, resolves PM pos → text offset, passes textOffset to
  context assembly; assembleContext() callback for skillExecutor does the
  same so both assembly calls are aligned

commit.message:
Noble access system, gift preferences, trade reputation

Noble social access gates:
- Can't talk/gift nobles without proper rank or introduction
- Minor Noble (4): need Guildmaster rank (3) or introduction
- Lord (5): need Minor Noble rank (4) or introduction
- Royal Advisor (6): need Lord rank (5) or introduction
- King: need Royal Advisor rank (6) or introduction from RA (lords only)
- Below Burgher: no introductions possible at all
- Observe/Ask Around still work on locked nobles
- Person detail shows lock status with explanation and Find Introduction button

Introduction system:
- Ask NPCs with 60+ relationship to introduce you to nobles
- ~33% of lower-rank NPCs actually know nobles (deterministic by ID hash)
- Success chance: 40% at rel 60, 70% at rel 80, 95% at rel 100
- 30-day cooldown per introducer after each request
- Same-rank introductions: ask known nobles to introduce peers
- Introduction grants starting relationship of 10
- Data: player.introductions, player.introductionCooldowns (saved/loaded)

Royal Counsel (advise king without petitions):
- Royal Advisors get 📜 Royal Counsel button next to existing advise
- Shows non-target petition types (lower/raise taxes)
- Success based on: king relationship + kingdom rep + king personality
- 7-day cooldown between advise actions

Gift preference system:
- Each NPC has a secret favorite gift (+10 rel) and hated gift (-5 rel)
- Preferences deterministic from NPC ID (no storage needed)
- Normal gifts: 1-8 relationship based on market value at location
- Giving favorite/hated auto-discovers that preference
- 10% chance to discover preference during social interactions
- 15% chance during observation
- Gift UI shows known preferences with 💖/😠 badges per item
- discoveredGiftPrefs saved/loaded

Trading builds town reputation:
- +0.1 reputation per 100g traded (buy or sell)
- Applied via modifyTownReputation so town size modifier applies

commit.message:
Fix deposit: pull from town storage, direct building-to-building transfer

Building deposit bug: player could sell wood at market (which merges
inventory + town storage) but couldn't deposit it into brick kiln
because deposit only checked personal inventory. Now depositToBuilding()
draws from inventory first, then town storage. Both deposit UIs
(building detail + storage modal) show combined inventory + town storage
with source labels.

Building-to-building transfer: _executeTransfer() now deposits directly
into target building's inventory instead of dumping to town market.
Smart ratio balancing for multi-input buildings (e.g. brick kiln needs
clay:3 + wood:1) — each input limited to its proportional capacity share
(+50% buffer). Overflow goes to town storage instead of being lost.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Land subsidy choice: subsidized (locked) vs full price land

When a town has an active royal land subsidy, clicking Buy Land now
shows a choice dialog: buy subsidized land (discounted but locked to
the specified building type) or buy regular land at full price.

- getLandCost() returns base price; getSubsidizedLandCost() includes discount
- buyLand(townId, useSubsidy) tracks locked plots in player.subsidizedLand
- buildBuilding() enforces lock: subsidized plots can only be used for
  the specified building type, consumed on build
- sellLand() sells subsidized plots first at subsidized resale value
- UI buttons show subsidy indicator when available
- Housing dialog shows locked plot details

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(deploy): make nginx listen on Railway's dynamic PORT via envsubst

Railway injects PORT env var — nginx must listen on it instead of hardcoded 80.
Use envsubst to replace \ in nginx template at container startup.
